title: Auth pitfalls on persistent connections
description: Long connections complicate auth: handshake-only checks, token expiry mid-stream, and cross-origin hijacking.
body: Use when securing a realtime endpoint. The handshake is the only standard place to authenticate, but the connection then outlives the token, so checking only at connect lets an expired or revoked token keep streaming. WebSockets are also not protected by the same-origin policy and ignore CORS, exposing cross-site hijacking if you authenticate via ambient cookies. Do not rely solely on cookies or a one-time handshake check. Actions: authenticate with a token, validate Origin, re-check authorization as tokens expire, and close on revocation. Validation signals include tests for expired-token streaming stopped and cross-origin connection rejected. Risk boundary: streaming private data past token expiry or accepting a cross-origin hijack is a security finding to escalate.
